questions.q: Near which Indian city is Ramoji Film City located?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City is a vast film studio complex situated near Hyderabad, in the state of Telangana.

questions.q: In which year was Ramoji Film City opened to the public?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City was opened in 1996 by media entrepreneur Ramoji Rao.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City is recognised by Guinness World Records for what distinction?
options: Most films shot in a single year
Largest film studio complex in the world
questions.explanation: Guinness World Records recognises Ramoji Film City as the largest film studio complex in the world.

questions.q: Which media entrepreneur founded Ramoji Film City?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City was founded by media entrepreneur Ramoji Rao, after whom it is named.

questions.q: Approximately how large is the Ramoji Film City complex?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City spans over 1,600 acres, making it the largest integrated film studio complex in the world.

questions.q: Which blockbuster film franchise directed by S. S. Rajamouli used Ramoji Film City extensively for its sets?
questions.explanation: The Baahubali films were shot extensively at Ramoji Film City, with many of their grand sets built there.

questions.q: Besides being a film studio, what other major role does Ramoji Film City serve?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City is also a popular tourism destination, drawing large numbers of visitors with its sets, gardens and attractions.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Ramoji Film City situated?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City lies near Hyderabad in the state of Telangana.

questions.q: Ramoji Rao, the founder of Ramoji Film City, was primarily associated with which industry beyond films?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao was a leading media baron, known for the Telugu newspaper Eenadu and the ETV television network.

questions.q: What type of attraction does Ramoji Film City offer to tourists who want to see how movies are made?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City offers studio tours and live film-making demonstrations that let visitors see sets and movie-making in action.

questions.q: Which Telugu newspaper, founded by Ramoji Rao, was the flagship of the media group behind Ramoji Film City?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao founded the influential Telugu daily Eenadu, the cornerstone of his media empire.

questions.q: The Ramoji Group that owns Ramoji Film City also launched which Telugu television network?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao's group launched the ETV network, a major Telugu and regional-language television brand.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City was designed as an 'integrated' studio, meaning it offers which capability?
options: End-to-end film production from sets to post-production under one roof
questions.explanation: As an integrated studio, Ramoji Film City provides pre-production, shooting and post-production facilities all within the complex.

questions.q: The Baahubali films shot at Ramoji Film City were directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: S. S. Rajamouli directed the Baahubali films, whose elaborate kingdom sets were built at Ramoji Film City.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City is part of the Ramoji Group, whose business empire was built by which single founder?
questions.explanation: The Ramoji Group, spanning Eenadu, ETV, Margadarsi and Ramoji Film City, was built by founder Ramoji Rao.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City contains numerous permanent 'sets' that replicate which kinds of environments?
options: Gardens, temples, palaces, and replica cityscapes
questions.explanation: The complex features a wide variety of permanent sets, including gardens, temples, palaces and replica streetscapes for filming.

questions.q: Roughly how many films can Ramoji Film City accommodate shooting simultaneously, a feature highlighting its scale?
questions.explanation: Its enormous size and many sets allow Ramoji Film City to host dozens of productions shooting simultaneously.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City is located on the outskirts of Hyderabad in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City lies on the south-eastern outskirts of Hyderabad, in the state of Telangana.

questions.q: Which honour did founder Ramoji Rao receive from the Government of India in 2016 for his contributions to journalism and education?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, in 2016.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City has a dedicated hospitality offering for visitors and crews. What is it?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City includes on-site hotels and resorts that host tourists as well as film crews during shoots.

questions.q: The Baahubali films, partly shot at Ramoji Film City, starred which actor in the title roles of Amarendra and Mahendra Baahubali?
questions.explanation: Prabhas played the dual title roles in the Baahubali films, whose sets were constructed at Ramoji Film City.

questions.q: In which year did Ramoji Film City first receive its Guinness World Records certification as the world's largest film studio complex?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City was certified by Guinness World Records as the world's largest film studio complex in 2005.

questions.q: The production designer who created the grand sets of Baahubali at Ramoji Film City was which acclaimed art director?
questions.explanation: Sabu Cyril was the production designer of the Baahubali films, creating their monumental sets at Ramoji Film City.

questions.q: In what year did Ramoji Rao, the founder of Ramoji Film City, pass away?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao died in June 2024 at the age of 87 in Hyderabad.

questions.q: Which Bengali art director, a multiple National Award winner, is credited with designing the sets and layout of Ramoji Film City?
questions.explanation: Art director Nitish Roy, brought in by Ramoji Rao, designed and laid out the themed sets of Ramoji Film City over several years.

questions.q: Before founding Ramoji Film City, Ramoji Rao established a chit-fund company in 1962. What was its name?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao founded Margadarsi Chit Fund in 1962, one of his early business ventures before his media and film enterprises.

questions.q: Ramoji Rao's film production house, which produced numerous Telugu films, was known by which name?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Rao's production banner Usha Kiron Movies produced many acclaimed Telugu films.

questions.q: Which 1985 Telugu biographical film, produced under Ramoji Rao's Usha Kiron Movies banner, told the real-life story of dancer Sudha Chandran?
questions.explanation: Usha Kiron Movies produced Mayuri (1985), the biographical film starring Sudha Chandran, for which she won a National Film Award Special Jury Award.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City is sometimes used to film television serials and reality shows in addition to feature films. This versatility is enabled mainly by what?
options: Its diverse standing sets and varied terrain across the campus
A government mandate restricting it to TV
Its location inside a city centre
questions.explanation: The vast campus offers diverse standing sets and varied terrain, letting it host feature films, TV serials and reality shows simultaneously.

questions.q: Ramoji Film City's blend of working studios with tourism is often compared to which famous overseas studio-and-theme-park model?
questions.explanation: Ramoji Film City blends working studios with theme-park tourism in a way often likened to Universal Studios.
